en_body:
Let others dress in robes proper and fine
One lamp one shadow, I dance with Poetry
Let others have their luxurious cities
I tend the white candle, burn incense of remembering sounds
The moon leans...leans
The night wind desolate
Stirs the flowered curtain while I chant drunken rhymes
Let others pursue their karmic destiny
Four walls beneath a numb roof
I hang my crimson heart
Hearing at last a far dog's barking
The soul of Poetry, cinnamon and cassia, homeland-love hot with blood
Two feet upon the level earth
The yearning heart turns homeward, offering a little Goodness to life
Let others bear rice-clothes debts
I wander freely through this mortal world
...seeking Poetry..!!
Let others fall face/up through luck/misfortune
I sit tapping the rhythm of True-Mind, calling the heart
Let others idle and dangle in midair
Floating clouds drift, the white colt's shadow beyond the sill
Let others turn aside on roads going/returning
Behind the locked window I wait for my Beloved.
note: The white colt's shadow is a classical image for the swift passing of time.
